title: The Giants Face a New Threat
description: On pages 7–9, this part follows Unknown person #7, Loki, World Government, Unknown person #8, Unknown person #9, King Harald as God’s Knights and Elbaf’s ruling tensions push the island toward open conflict while the Straw Hats and giants react to the threat.
scene: God’s Knights and Elbaf’s ruling tensions push the island toward open conflict while the Straw Hats and giants react to the threat.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Don't speak a word...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: of what happened here today!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If we say nothing, who do you think they'll blame for all this?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: It'll be the same as always!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Say I were to become King, can you see me actually honoring Father's ideals?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I can only tackle this... in my own way!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: With this ability!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll settle the regrets of the old man and his soldiers.

dialogue.speaker: Loki
dialogue.text: Don't be stupid, Loki!!

dialogue.speaker: World Government
dialogue.text: The world government is a big organization!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It won't be as easy as you think!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: something big happened!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Quest Castle was attacked!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Your Highness!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: After that, I set out to sea.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hajordin, by the time you guys returned to the island...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: "Everyone had already dubbed me "Lok! The Patriciaide,"

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And I made no effort to correct that narrative.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: But...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's NOT LIKE I WAS CRAZY ENOUGH TO BRAG ABOUT KILLING HIM EITHER!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I just didn't want everything he stood for to be dismissed...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: ...because of how things turned out!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: With all that in mind...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Do you really think I should have told the people everything?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: He's telling the truth, Rajrudin.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Get it already? I really didn't want to relive all this crap!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Elder JARUL...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: ...Always opposed to your execution.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I can understand why you chose to keep this secret...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: father choosing death in the face of the government's manipulation...

dialogue.speaker: World Government
dialogue.text: The World Government has been...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: ...OUR ENEMY ALL THIS TIME!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I wonder... What did they do to father?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Indeed... and now, they've sent more knights...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...to Elbaf. Some with deep ties to Harald.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: even lol doesn't know all the details..

dialogue.speaker: King Harald
dialogue.text: _About Harald's dealings in the Holy Land.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I can fill you in if you want.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: that won't be necessary for now, ya-san.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We need no further reason to protect our country!!

summary: This part advances God’s Knights Tighten Their Grip through 40 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
